description_long: This sculpture depicts a seated female figure, likely of religious or historical significance, holding a smaller child figure on her lap. The figures are carved from wood and display a weathered, aged surface with remnants of dark brown and gold paint, particularly around the neckline and head. The adult figure has a serene, elongated face with stylized hair and a crown or headdress, while the child is smaller, centrally positioned, and also features a rounded head. The sculpture is mounted on a simple, dark rectangular base for stability. The back of the sculpture is less detailed, showing the rough, unfinished wood and some visible wear. The overall form is vertical and compact, with the figures closely integrated into a single block.
description: A weathered wooden sculpture of a seated woman holding a child, with traces of dark and gold paint and mounted on a rectangular base.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing the detailed faces and postures of both the woman and child, with visible gold accents and the full seated composition.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the profile of the figures, the depth of the carving, and the layered drapery of the woman's robe.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the rough, unfinished back of the sculpture and the mounting support attached to the base.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the profile of the figures, the child's position on the lap, and the curvature of the woman's back.
attribute_subject_matter: seated female figure holding a child
attribute_location_context: No specific installation context is visible; the sculpture is shown isolated on a plain background.
